result:
# Introducing Smart Drafts: Never Start From Zero Again

We've all felt it — that quiet dread of opening a fresh document and watching the cursor blink back. Whether it's a project brief, a meeting recap, or a quarterly review, the hardest part is rarely the writing itself. It's the staring. We built Smart Drafts because we kept hearing the same thing from you: the ideas are there, but the runway to get going feels too long. So we set out to shorten it.

Starting today, every new document includes a one-click Smart Draft button. Give your doc a title, hit the button, and you'll get a thoughtful outline tailored to what you're working on — informed by your recent docs, shared context from your workspace, and the patterns of how your team tends to structure similar work. It's not a finished draft, and it's not meant to be. Think of it as a generous first sketch: headers in the right places, prompts where you'd want them, and just enough scaffolding to make the next sentence feel obvious. Edit freely, ignore what doesn't fit, or regenerate with a nudge in a different direction.

This is the first step in a longer arc. Next up: custom voice training, so Smart Drafts don't just match the shape of your work but the sound of it — your phrasing, your cadence, the way your team actually writes. We'll have more to share on that in the coming weeks. In the meantime, open a new doc and give Smart Drafts a try. We'd love to hear what you think.

# Introducing Smart Drafts: Never Start From Zero Again

We've all felt it — that quiet dread of opening a fresh document and watching the cursor blink back. Whether it's a project brief, a meeting recap, or a quarterly review, the hardest part is rarely the writing itself. It's the staring. We built Smart Drafts because we kept hearing the same thing from you: the ideas are there, but the runway to get going feels too long. So we set out to shorten it.

Starting today, every new document includes a one-click Smart Draft button. Give your doc a title, hit the button, and you'll get a thoughtful outline tailored to what you're working on — informed by your recent docs, shared context from your workspace, and the patterns of how your team tends to structure similar work. It's not a finished draft, and it's not meant to be. Think of it as a generous first sketch: headers in the right places, prompts where you'd want them, and just enough scaffolding to make the next sentence feel obvious. Edit freely, ignore what doesn't fit, or regenerate with a nudge in a different direction.

This is the first step in a longer arc. Next up: custom voice training, so Smart Drafts don't just match the shape of your work but the sound of it — your phrasing, your cadence, the way your team actually writes. We'll have more to share on that in the coming weeks. In the meantime, open a new doc and give Smart Drafts a try. We'd love to hear what you think.
